Staff Guide

Skyla's AndroidAPS Guide

How the closed-loop system works, and what you need to do at mealtimes

How It Works The App Entering Carbs Bolusing Strategy Viewing Treatments The Watch
🔄

How the Closed-Loop System Works

Three components working together

Skyla's diabetes is managed by a closed-loop system. The glucose sensor reads her blood sugar every 5 minutes, sends it to the AndroidAPS app on her phone, which then automatically tells her insulin pump how much insulin to give.

You don't need to adjust the automatic insulin. Your main job is to tell the app about carbohydrates when Skyla eats.

Dexcom G7 Sensor

Worn on Skyla's upper arm. Reads blood glucose every 5 minutes via Bluetooth to her phone. Changed every 10 days.

AndroidAPS App

On Skyla's phone (passcode: 1013). Receives sensor data and decides insulin doses. Needs carb information from you at mealtimes.

Dana-i Insulin Pump

Small device on Skyla's tummy. Delivers insulin automatically via a thin tube. Do not remove it.

⚠️ Important: CGM readings are approximately 10–15 minutes behind a finger-prick reading. If blood glucose is rising or dropping rapidly, the actual value may be several points further along than shown.
📱

The AndroidAPS App — Home Screen

  • Large number Current blood glucose (mmol/L). Green = in range, yellow/red = outside range.
  • Arrow Direction of travel. → stable, ↗ rising, ↘ falling, ⇊ falling fast.
  • Δ values Change in last 5, 15, and 40 minutes.
  • Syringe icon Insulin on board (IOB) — active insulin still working.
  • Wheat icon Carbs on board (COB) — carbs still being absorbed.
  • T:0% Temporary basal rate. T:0% means pump is paused (e.g. during exercise).
  • Graph Blood glucose over time. Green zone = target range (4–10). Dotted line = prediction.
  • Bottom buttons INSULIN (manual dose), CARBS (enter carbs only), CALCULATOR (use this for meals).
📲 Phone passcode: 1013. Skyla's phone must stay within 6 feet of her at all times so the sensor can communicate with the app.
🌾

Entering a Mealtime Bolus (Carbs)

When Skyla eats, you must enter the carb amount into the AndroidAPS Calculator. The app will calculate the right insulin dose and send it to the pump automatically.

1 From the phone home screen, tap the AndroidAPS widget (shows blood glucose) or open the AndroidAPS app.
2 Tap the CALCULATOR button (bottom right). Enter the carb amount using the + next to Carbs. Change carb time to 15 mins, then tap OK.
3 A confirmation screen shows the calculated insulin dose and carbs. Tap OK to confirm delivery.
🍽️

Bolusing Strategy

Rule of thumb for lunch: Enter 30–40g upfront (or less if fewer carbs on her plate), then add the remainder once you know what she has eaten. If confident she will eat more (e.g. fish and chip Fridays), you can increase the upfront bolus to a maximum of 50g.

In an ideal world, weigh the food and enter the bolus 5–10 minutes before she eats. In practice, entering upfront and topping up afterwards works well. Always keep a banana on standby in case she doesn't eat her meal after a big bolus.

🍝 Pasta — Special Rule
Pasta releases carbohydrates slowly over 2–4 hours. Giving the full bolus upfront will likely cause a hypo. Split the bolus in two: give the first half upfront, and the second half after around 90–120 minutes. For example: 30g at 12:00, then 20g at 13:30.
📋

Viewing Treatments

If you want to review what insulin and carbs have been given, you can check the Treatments log.

1 On the home screen, tap the three dots (⋮) in the top-right corner.
2 Tap Treatments from the menu.
3 The Treatments screen shows a full record of all carbs entered (in orange/green) and insulin delivered (in white/blue), with timestamps.
⌚

The The Watch

Skyla will bring a watch for the supervising adult to wear. It shows her blood glucose at a glance without needing to check the phone.

Display elementWhat it means
Large number (top)Current blood glucose in mmol/L. Target range: 4.0–10.0.
Arrows (top right)Trend direction. ↓↓ = falling fast and may need treatment soon.
Small number (top left)Minutes since last reading. Should update every 5 min. If over 15 min, check Skyla has her phone nearby.
Change value (e.g. −1.1)Change in blood glucose since last reading.
GraphBlood glucose over the last few hours. Red line = hypo threshold (4.0). Yellow line = upper target.
🚨 Treat if blood sugar drops below 4.0 or the arrows show it is falling fast toward 4.0. See the separate Quick Reference card for hypo treatment steps.
